raw_output_payload.reasoning: Need to print all files and directories recursively including hidden. Use find. Command: find /system/folder2 -print. To include hidden, find automatically includes. But to show hidden files in directories, need to use -a? Actually find will list all. So answer: find /system/folder2 -print. Or ls -R? But hidden files start with dot; ls -R will not show hidden unless -a. So: ls -R -a /system/folder2. But requirement: recursively print all files and directories. find is better. Provide command.
